setupGuide.notes: Placeholder for Bahrain International Circuit - 57 laps / 308.5 km reproduced as 67 laps of the modern Hockenheim GP. AMS2 has no Bahrain; the redesigned Tilke Hockenheim GP is a same-era, same-designer wide-modern-GP stand-in.

Placeholder for Sepang International Circuit - 56 laps / 310.4 km reproduced as 68 laps of the modern Hockenheim GP. Both are Hermann Tilke designs; the redesigned Hockenheim GP keeps Sepang's long-straight-into-hairpin rhythm (same stand-in as the f1-2000 pack).

Placeholder for Melbourne Grand Prix Circuit - 57 laps / 302.3 km reproduced as 94 laps of Adelaide Modern. Modern Adelaide keeps the Australian parkland-circuit character (same stand-in as the f1-2000 pack).

track.realVenue: Autodromo Internazionale Enzo e Dino Ferrari

setupGuide.notes: AMS2's Imola 2005 is the closest layout to the 2006 configuration; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
The 2006 European GP ran the Nürburgring GP-Strecke; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the modern GP layout.
No 2006 Barcelona layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-close pre-2007 layout.
AMS2's modern Azure Circuit matches the 2006 (post-2004 reprofile) Monaco layout; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
No 2006 Silverstone in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the near-identical 2001 layout.
2006 Montreal is the same venue as Montreal Modern, so the historical lap count is kept.
The 2006 US GP ran the Indianapolis road course; AMS2's Indianapolis 2001 road course is that same layout, so the historical lap count is kept.

Placeholder for Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours - 70 laps / 308.8 km reproduced as 74 laps of Le Mans Circuit Bugatti. Keeps the French GP at a French Grand Prix venue (same stand-in as the f1-2000 and f1-1988 packs). Montoya's McLaren #4 seat passed to the unbindable Pedro de la Rosa from this round, so the grid is one car lighter.
The 2006 German GP ran the modern (2002+) Hockenheim GP; AMS2's Hockenheim GP is that same layout, so the historical lap count is kept. Sakon Yamamoto joins the Super Aguri #23 seat from this round.

AMS2 ships the 2025 Hungaroring; the venue is real and largely unchanged in character since 1986, so the historical lap count is kept. Villeneuve's BMW Sauber #17 seat passed to the unbindable Robert Kubica from this round, so the grid is one car lighter.

Placeholder for Istanbul Park - 58 laps / 309.4 km reproduced as 66 laps of Barcelona GP. AMS2 has no Istanbul; the flowing Tilke-designed Barcelona GP is a same-era stand-in with a comparable high-speed-corner character.
AMS2's Monza 2005 is the 2006 layout; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.

Placeholder for Shanghai International Circuit - 56 laps / 305.3 km reproduced as 65 laps of Barcelona GP. AMS2 has no Shanghai; the Tilke-designed Barcelona GP is a same-era wide-modern-GP stand-in. Klien's Red Bull #15 seat passed to the unbindable Robert Doornbos from this round, so the grid is one car lighter.
Suzuka appears in AMS2 as Kansai; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the GP layout.
No 2006 Interlagos layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-close 1993 layout.
